directions.name: Two-level B staging for the 64x384 hot band
directions.hypothesis: The promoted 64x384 path already wins on CTA count, but the B feed still uses one row-major shared layout plus a single 16-byte warp-group skew. On the measured 64x384 kernel, mio_throttle is 31.60%, l1tex LSU wavefront pressure is high, and tensor active is only 32.61%, which points to the current B producer and consumer layout serving neither cp.async/coalescing nor warp consumption especially well. Keep the 64x384 outer tile, but split global->shared and shared->fragment into separate layouts via a CTA-local reorder or an interleaved swizzle so producer traffic and consumer bank behavior are optimized independently.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Shared/L1 B-feed pressure and LSU issue bandwidth in the hot 64x384 kernel.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:47-52 (current single-skew B shared layout in TensorCoreTileConfig)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:208-239 (logical-to-shared B mapping and async staging path)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:437-445 (warp B base pointer selection and per-fragment shared loads)
directions.risk: A two-level B layout can add address math, extra shared traffic, or enough staging footprint to offset the gain if the reorder is not kept local and cheap.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if mio_throttle and L1/shared-bank pressure stay flat, or if runtime regresses without a clear rise in tensor active.

directions.name: Phased 64x384 micro-panels to shrink the live set
directions.hypothesis: Autotune says 64x384 is the best outer CTA width, but the current inner schedule keeps TileConfig::kWarpMmaTilesN=12 live across the full K loop, so each warp carries 12 accumulator fragments and 12 B fragments at once. That matches the measured 126 registers/thread and register-limited 2-block occupancy. Preserve the 64x384 outer tile and launch geometry, but execute it as serial micro-panels such as 2x192 or 3x128 so the live B and accumulator set is smaller while the CTA still covers 384 columns overall.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register-limited occupancy and weak latency hiding from keeping the full 384-wide working set live per warp.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:32-39 (TileConfig shape parameters, especially kWarpMmaTilesN and kTensorBlockN)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:406-445 (accumulator and B-fragment live set inside the hot MMA loop)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:517-550 (fixed-shape launch path that should keep the 64x384 outer tile intact)
directions.risk: Serial micro-panels can give back some of the 64x384 CTA-count win if extra panel boundaries, reloads, or control flow add too much instruction overhead.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if registers/thread stays near 126 or occupancy remains capped at 2 resident CTAs without a runtime gain.

directions.name: Warp-specialized producer-consumer pipeline for the 64x384 loop
directions.hypothesis: The current 64x384 kernel has moderate barrier stall (8.93%) on top of heavy mio_throttle (31.60%), while DRAM throughput is only 27.74%. That suggests issue slots are being spent on CTA-wide stage orchestration rather than pure math. Keep the 64x384 tile, but assign a subset of warps to producer duties for A/B cp.async staging and let the remaining warps stay focused on MMA so the steady-state loop spends fewer cycles in all-warp copy/setup plus block-wide synchronization.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Synchronization and stage-orchestration overhead in the hot K-loop rather than raw DRAM bandwidth.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:213-239 (current all-warps async staging helpers for A and B)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:392-404 (warp role derivation and tile ownership)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:421-450 (current steady-state loop, cp.async commits, waits, and CTA barriers)
directions.risk: Dedicating warps to producer work can lower compute-side parallelism if overlap is weak, and the extra role specialization may complicate correctness or make the copy warps the new bottleneck.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if barrier stall does not fall meaningfully or if runtime regresses because the producer warps starve the consumer warps.

notes: Round 1/5 diagnosis prepared from run 20260419_015554_bf16_gemm_v1_16a98f7. Recommended dir_01 per human-priority override; dir_01 and dir_02 are human ideas.
